title: Song: We Don't Eat Before Dawn
title_en: Song: We Don't Eat Before Dawn
objectives: Reinforce present simple negative (don't / doesn't) through song
Practise Ramadan-related vocabulary in a fun context

key_concepts.concept: Song Lyrics — We Don't Eat Before Dawn
key_concepts.explanation:
Verse 1: It's Ramadan — a special time, / We fast and pray, our hearts all shine. / We don't eat before the dawn, / We wait until the night comes on.

Chorus: We don't eat, we don't drink, / Before the dawn — stop and think! / After sunset, we can eat, / Dates and soup — what a treat!

Verse 2: He doesn't eat, she doesn't too, / We all fast, the whole day through. / After sunset, lights go on, / We thank Allah — the day is done.

(Chorus repeats)

teaching_notes: This song reinforces the grammar point (don't/doesn't) and Ramadan vocabulary from the unit. Play the audio via QR code. The chorus is catchy and repeats — good for whole-class singing. The song uses both 'don't' (I/we) and 'doesn't' (he/she), reinforcing the grammar distinction. Key vocabulary reinforced: Ramadan, fast, dawn, sunset, pray, eat, drink, dates, soup.
